comment_text.sn5.9:1.2: As noted, the verses of Selā at Thig 3.7 are attributed to Āḷavikā at SN 5.1. The verses here have no Pali parallels, and it is unclear whether this is the same person.
comment_text.sn5.9:2.1: “Puppet” is bimba; the similarity to English “bimbo” seems a coincidence.
comment_text.sn5.9:4.1: This contradicts eg. Bṛhadāraṇyaka Upaniṣad 4.3.10, “for he is the maker”.
comment_text.sn5.9:4.2: Neither of the two Chinese parallels at SA2 219 or SA 1203 contain any parallel to agha (“gloom”).
comment_text.sn5.9:5.1: Selā uses the imagery of agriculture to illustrate the organic, changing nature of an individual, who survives supported by multiple conditions.

root_text.sn5.9:1.2: Atha kho selā bhikkhunī pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā …pe…
root_text.sn5.9:1.4: Atha kho māro pāpimā selāya bhikkhuniyā bhayaṁ chambhitattaṁ lomahaṁsaṁ uppādetukāmo …pe…
root_text.sn5.9:3.2: “ko nu khvāyaṁ manusso vā amanusso vā gāthaṁ bhāsatī”ti?
root_text.sn5.9:3.4: “māro kho ayaṁ pāpimā mama bhayaṁ chambhitattaṁ lomahaṁsaṁ uppādetukāmo samādhimhā cāvetukāmo gāthaṁ bhāsatī”ti.
root_text.sn5.9:3.5: Atha kho selā bhikkhunī “māro ayaṁ pāpimā” iti viditvā māraṁ pāpimantaṁ gāthāhi paccabhāsi:
root_text.sn5.9:7.1: Atha kho māro pāpimā “jānāti maṁ selā bhikkhunī”ti dukkhī dummano tatthevantaradhāyīti.

translation_text.sn5.9:1.2: Then the nun Selā robed up in the morning …
translation_text.sn5.9:1.3: and sat at the root of a tree for the day’s meditation.
translation_text.sn5.9:1.4: Then Māra the Wicked, wanting to make the nun Selā feel fear, terror, and goosebumps …
translation_text.sn5.9:3.2: “Who’s speaking this verse, a human or a non-human?”
translation_text.sn5.9:3.4: “This is Māra the Wicked, wanting to make me feel fear, terror, and goosebumps, wanting to make me fall away from immersion!”
translation_text.sn5.9:3.5: Then Selā, knowing that this was Māra the Wicked, replied to him in verse:
translation_text.sn5.9:4.2: nor is this gloom made by another.
translation_text.sn5.9:4.3: It comes to be because of a cause,
translation_text.sn5.9:4.4: and ceases when the cause breaks up.
translation_text.sn5.9:5.1: It’s like a seed that’s sown
translation_text.sn5.9:5.3: relying on both the soil’s nutrients
translation_text.sn5.9:6.1: In the same way the aggregates and elements
translation_text.sn5.9:6.3: come to be because of a cause,
translation_text.sn5.9:6.4: and cease when the cause breaks up.”
translation_text.sn5.9:7.1: Then Māra the Wicked, thinking, “The nun Selā knows me!” miserable and sad, vanished right there.

variant_text.sn5.9:2.2: kvanu → kvannu (bj, pts1ed, pts2ed); kvaci (sya-all, km, mr)
